description: Static audit rules for the Tab component. Rules are checked against rendered HTML. All rule IDs are globally unique within the platform audit system.

rules.title: Tablist must have an accessible label
rules.description: Every element with role='tablist' must have either aria-label or aria-labelledby. Without a label, screen readers cannot identify the tab group.
rules.fix: Add aria-label='[Group name]' or aria-labelledby='[heading-id]' to the .tab-list container.

rules.title: Tab button must have role='tab' and aria-selected
rules.description: Each tab button must declare role='tab' and aria-selected='true|false'. Missing either attribute breaks the ARIA Tabs pattern — assistive technology cannot identify tabs or their state.
rules.fix: Add role='tab' and aria-selected='true' (or 'false') to every .tab button.
rules.example_pass: <button class="tab tab--h" role="tab" aria-selected="true" aria-controls="panel-1" id="tab-1">

rules.title: Tab panel must have role='tabpanel' and aria-labelledby matching the tab id
rules.description: Each panel must declare role='tabpanel', an id, and aria-labelledby referencing the id of the controlling tab. This establishes the programmatic relationship between tab and panel for screen readers.

check.cross_check: aria-labelledby must match the id of an element with role='tab' that has aria-controls matching this element's id

rules.fix: Add role='tabpanel', id='panel-id', and aria-labelledby='tab-id' to each panel. Ensure the tab's aria-controls='panel-id' and id='tab-id' match.

rules.title: Tab button must suppress native :focus outline
rules.description: tab.css sets outline:none on .tab and uses a custom ::after focus ring instead. If native outline is not suppressed, double focus indicators will appear on keyboard navigation.

check.fail_condition: outline is not none in base state

rules.fix: Ensure tab.css is loaded and applies outline:none to .tab. Do not override the custom ::after focus ring via additional stylesheets.

rules.title: Tablist should have Arrow key navigation implemented in JavaScript
rules.description: WCAG 2.1.1 requires that all functionality be keyboard operable. The WAI-ARIA APG Tabs pattern mandates Arrow key navigation between tabs. CSS alone cannot implement this. Flag if no keyboard navigation script is detected.

check.fail_condition: no JavaScript keyboard event listener or data hook detected

rules.fix: Add data-tabs to the tab group wrapper. Implement a keydown listener on the tablist that handles ArrowRight/Left (horizontal) or ArrowUp/Down (vertical), Home, and End.

rules.title: Only the selected tab should have tabindex='0'
rules.description: The roving tabindex pattern requires exactly one tab per tablist to have tabindex='0' (the selected tab). All other tabs must have tabindex='-1'. This ensures only one Tab stop enters the tablist while Arrow keys move between tabs.

check.rule: within a role='tablist', exactly one role='tab' should have tabindex='0' (or no tabindex); all others should have tabindex='-1'. That one must be aria-selected='true'.
check.fail_condition: multiple tabs with tabindex=0, or selected tab has tabindex=-1

rules.fix: Set tabindex='-1' on all non-selected tabs. The selected tab should have tabindex='0' (or omit tabindex). JavaScript must update tabindex values on selection change.

rules.title: Vertical tablist must declare aria-orientation='vertical'
rules.description: Without aria-orientation='vertical', screen readers default to treating the tablist as horizontal, announcing incorrect keyboard navigation instructions (ArrowLeft/Right instead of ArrowUp/Down) to the user.

rules.title: Tab must use <button> element
rules.description: The .tab class must be applied to a <button> element. Using <div>, <a>, <span>, or any other element breaks keyboard access, native form semantics, and the ARIA Tabs pattern. <a> tags are only valid if tabs navigate to separate pages — in that case, use a different component.
rules.fix: Change the element to <button type='button'>. Never use <div role='button'> or <a> for a tab button.

rules.title: Selected state must be driven by aria-selected, not a CSS class
rules.description: tab.css uses [aria-selected='true'] as the CSS hook for selected appearance. Using a class like .is-selected or .active creates a gap between visual state and accessibility state — the component would appear selected to sighted users but not to assistive technology.
rules.fix: Remove any class-based selected state. Use aria-selected='true' only. JavaScript should set aria-selected on state change.

rules.title: Tab must have both an orientation and a base class
rules.description: A .tab button must always have exactly one orientation modifier: .tab--h or .tab--v. The base .tab class alone provides no sizing, color, or indicator positioning. Using both .tab--h and .tab--v simultaneously is prohibited.

check.fail_condition: neither or both orientation modifiers present

rules.fix: Add exactly one orientation modifier: .tab--h for horizontal tabs, .tab--v for vertical tabs.

rules.title: No hardcoded visual values in component selectors
rules.description: Component CSS selectors must not contain hardcoded colors, font sizes, padding values, or border radii. All visual values must reference --tab-* CSS custom properties. Hardcoded values are only permitted in the :root token block (as fallbacks) until those tokens are migrated to token.css.

check.fail_condition: hardcoded color or dimension in a non-:root selector

rules.fix: Replace hardcoded values with --tab-* tokens. If the token does not exist in token.css, add it to the :root block in tab.css with a primitive fallback and note a TODO comment.

rules.title: No physical left/right properties in base tab selectors
rules.description: Using left or right in base selectors (.tab, .tab--h, .tab--v, .tab-list--h, .tab-list--v) without a [dir='rtl'] counterpart breaks RTL layouts. Use logical properties (padding-inline-start, inset-inline-start) or always pair physical properties with an RTL override.

check.fail_condition: physical directional property used without a matching [dir='rtl'] override

rules.fix: Pair each `left` with `[dir='rtl'] ... { left: auto; right: ...; }` or switch to logical CSS properties (`inset-inline-start`).

rules.title: Icons inside tabs must use aria-hidden='true'
rules.description: Icon SVG or wrapper elements inside .tab must be hidden from screen readers. In RTL, flex-direction: row-reverse visually moves the icon, but the icon has no accessible meaning — it must be aria-hidden='true' so screen readers announce only the label text.
rules.fix: Add aria-hidden='true' to .tab__icon and to any <svg> directly inside a tab.

rules.title: Multiple tabs with aria-selected='true' in one tablist
rules.description: Exactly one tab per tablist should be selected at any time. Multiple tabs with aria-selected='true' violates the ARIA Tabs pattern and produces confusing screen reader announcements.

check.fail_condition: 0 or more than 1 selected tabs per tablist

rules.fix: Ensure exactly one tab has aria-selected='true' and all others have aria-selected='false' in each tablist.

rules.title: Tab orientation modifier must match parent tab-list orientation modifier
rules.description: A .tab--h button inside a .tab-list--v (or vice versa) produces misaligned indicator positioning, wrong padding, and broken CSS specificity. The orientation modifier on the tab must match the container.

check.rule: .tab-list--h children must use .tab--h; .tab-list--v children must use .tab--v
check.fail_condition: orientation mismatch between .tab-list and its .tab children

rules.fix: Use .tab--h inside .tab-list--h. Use .tab--v inside .tab-list--v. Never mix.

rules.title: Tab should not be used for primary site navigation
rules.description: The Tab component uses ARIA Tabs pattern (tablist/tab/tabpanel). It is designed for switching between related content sections on the same page. Using it for site-level navigation (header links, sidebar navs) is semantically incorrect and violates WAI-ARIA usage intent.

check.fail_condition: tablist is inside a navigation landmark

rules.fix: For site navigation, use a Nav component or a <nav> element with <a> links instead of Tab.
